chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Crl. Bail Appln No.S-698 of 2025 DATE OF HEARING ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE. 1. For orders on M.A. No.4185/25 2. For orders on M.A. No.4186/25 3. For hearing of bail application. 15.08.2025 Mr. Danish Ali Bhatti, Advocate alongwith applicant. ******** 1. Urgency allowed. 2. Granted, subject to all just exceptions. 3. Through instant application, applicant Muhammad Anas son of Karim Bux Bhutto seeks protective bail in Crime No.18/2025 Police Station, “Civil Lines” district Larkana under Sections 489-F PPC. It is, inter alia, contended that applicant is innocent and has falsely been involved in this case by the complainant with malafide intention and ulterior motives. It is further contended that as soon as applicant came to know he tried to surrender himself before the concerned Court for pre-arrest bail but applicant has grave apprehension of his being arrest before reaching the same at the hands of concerned police hence, he has approached this Court for grant of protective bail. Be that as it may, without touching merits and demerits of the case and in order to enable applicant to appear before concerned Court, he is granted protective bail for a period of 07(Seven) days commencing from today, subject to furnishing his solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty thousand) and PR bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court. This order shall cease to have its effect on expiry of said period or on appearance of applicant before concerned Court, whichever is earlier. In case, applicant fails to surrender before the learned trial Court within stipulated period, his bail bond shall stand forfeited.

This order shall cease to have its effect on expiry of said period or on appearance of applicant before concerned Court, whichever is earlier. In case, applicant fails to surrender before the learned trial Court within stipulated period, his bail bond shall stand forfeited. Bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. J U D G E Ihsan/PS
